paper_title: Simulation and Analysis of Microspines Interlocking Behavior on Rocky Surfaces: An In-Depth Study of the Isolated Spine
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/05_Simulation and analysis of microspines interlocking behavior on rocky surfaces An in-depth study of.pdf
sections_checked: 5.4 Influence of KIIc and R on the Mean Force Function
6.1 Rock Roughness Estimation and Tip Measurement Analysis

equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1 exactly as printed: tau_cr = K_IIc sqrt(pi c). A 600-dpi visual recheck confirms multiplication, not division. The printed equation is dimensionally inconsistent with tau_cr as stress and K_IIc in MPa sqrt(m); it is therefore marked unusable pending erratum/code/re-derivation.
Retained as E2; F = tau_cr A was visually checked. The surrounding text states that this is the force component parallel to the fracture plane.
Retained as E3; alpha = atan(1/mu_s) and the Fig. 2 angle geometry were visually checked.
Retained as E4; both exponential factors, Ra-t term and wd term were visually checked. The authors' d=0 and Ra<t caveats are preserved.
Visually verified as F = exp[-c(Ra)](a + b sqrt(d)); omitted as a separate retained formula because Eq. (6) is the paper's K_IIc-linearized final estimator.
Retained as E5; factor 10 K_IIc, exponential exp[-p(Ra)] and m+n sqrt(d) were visually checked. Parameter units are not reported, so the fit is not directly portable.

tables_checked.notes: Ra, R and K_IIc simulation ranges checked.
Fixed friction, elastic and strength inputs checked. The final traction-strength symbol is typographically unclear and was not retained.
v, w, t and r-square values for R=5/10/50 micrometers checked and retained.
Eq. (5) fit parameters checked; omitted from the evidence card because Eq. (6)/Table 5 is the final reduced estimator.
K-independent Eq. (6) fit parameters checked and reproduced only for exact-unit replication; their units are absent and direct transfer is rejected.
Unused and worn tip radii 13.2, 33.81 and 32.88 micrometers checked.
p_lock, measured mean force, simulated force and Hertz force columns checked. Rock A/C measured-versus-simulated values and Rock D/E coating contrast are retained.

figures_checked.reason: Hertz stress trend is compactly preserved as negative evidence; its plotting inputs are not fully specified for reuse.
Irreplaceable 2D contact, fracture-angle, fracture-plane and fracture-length geometry; crop visually verified with labels and caption.
Experimental rig details are adequately summarized by loading direction, compliance constraint and spine angle.
Substrate photographs do not add quantitative evidence beyond the material/cover descriptions.
The fitted probability surface is represented more precisely by Eq. (4), Table 3 and its domain warnings.
Residual trend is noted through reported r-square range; no additional reusable parameter is visible.
Direct visual comparison of simulated force data, Log-Normal and Gaussian models; paired with Fig. 12 for the minimal simulation-experiment chain.
Mean-force surface and residuals are adequately represented by Eq. (5)-(6), fit ranges and limitations.
Tip-radius trend is retained textually; the plot is an empirical surface without portable coefficients beyond the tables.
Approximate K_IIc proportionality is preserved with Eq. (6) and its limited-domain warning.
The worn-tip image is replaced by the measured radii in Table 6.
Minimal experimental evidence that the force distribution remains right-skewed and is represented by a Log-Normal curve; crop visually verified.

omitted_sections: General application and robot-history background in the Introduction
Detailed rig hardware and sample photographs not affecting interpretation

uncertain_items.item: Dimensional inconsistency in the printed Mode-II critical-stress formula
uncertain_items.detail: The PDF clearly prints tau_cr = K_IIc sqrt(pi c), while K_IIc is later tabulated in MPa sqrt(m). The right-hand side is not a stress. The intended equation cannot be corrected from this paper alone.
The last row appears with a nu-like symbol although the quantity is in MPa and the text discusses strength. It was omitted because it is not required by the retained mechanism.
The paper gives numerical m, n and p values but does not report their units; Eq. (6) is therefore not portable outside the exact unit convention and fit domain.
Ra=30 micrometers and K_IIc values are estimated by analogy to prior literature rather than measured on the tested samples; only order-of-magnitude comparison is claimed.

The paper reports p_lock and force distributions separately but does not state clearly whether failed or near-zero trials were excluded before the Log-Normal fits. A Bernoulli-plus-positive-distribution interpretation is therefore a downstream modeling choice, not an explicit paper result.

known_warnings: The fracture-plane model is two-dimensional and approximates area as squared fracture length; it is not a validated three-dimensional crack model.
Eq. (4) must be clipped for Ra<t and does not allow nonzero capture at zero nominal travel.
The simulated model overestimates measured forces for all quantitative Rock A/C comparisons in Table 7.
The experimental rig has nearly zero normal preload and near-zero horizontal compliance, unlike a compliant microspine module.
Single-spine Bernoulli and Log-Normal marginals are not evidence of independent array behavior or load sharing.
Rendered pages emitted Poppler font warnings for Symbol/ArialUnicode, but every page, equation, table and retained crop was visually inspected and all relevant glyphs were legible.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains fracture, capture-probability and force-distribution submodels.
